dimensions.rationale: Revision 16 changes only release ownership for publication manifests and version-preparation governance; it does not change lifecycle behavior or adapter maturity.
Root publication manifests and the shared publication-manifest test are assigned to WS-14. Adapter-local projection files remain under their existing adapter workstreams.
The required post-refreeze gates are package tests, neutrality scan, ownership audit, release assembly and release verification before tag creation.
The release remains an EXPERIMENTAL source release and does not claim public marketplace approval, VERIFIED adapter maturity, or production promotion.

findings.message: Root publication manifests required for versioned source release preparation were not assigned in the frozen write-set contract.
findings.requiredAction: Assign .codex-plugin, .claude-plugin, .cursor-plugin and .agents/plugins to WS-14 release engineering and rerun ownership gates.

findings.message: The shared publication-manifest test validates release-governance artifacts but was not assigned to a release owner.
findings.requiredAction: Assign tests/adapters/test_publication_manifests.py to WS-14 and keep adapter-specific tests under their adapter workstreams.

summary: Revision 16 is READY_TO_FREEZE as a minimal release-version ownership correction for v0.1.2 preparation. It changes no product scope, task DAG, runtime behavior, adapter maturity, or production-promotion boundary.
